source: NvChad - Installing Nerd Fonts: what Nerd Fonts are and how to install them on Rocky Linux

options: Programming fonts patched with extra glyphs/icons (Font Awesome, Devicons, etc.)
questions.why: Nerd Fonts add iconic-font glyphs to popular programming fonts.

questions.q: Why would you use the Nerd Fonts font patcher?
options: To add the icon glyphs to a font that has not already been patched
questions.why: The patcher adds glyphs to fonts that are not pre-patched.

questions.q: The Rocky Linux install method uses git plus which tool to register fonts?
questions.why: install.sh copies the fonts then calls fc-cache.

questions.q: Why does the guide use a sparse, blob:none clone of the nerd-fonts repo?
options: So you download only the fonts you want, not every patched font
questions.why: Sparse checkout avoids pulling the huge patched-fonts tree.

questions.q: After ./install.sh IBMPlexMono, the font is renamed to BlexMono because...
options: of the SIL Open Font License (OFL) reserved-name mechanism
IBM trademarks block the original name
of a typo in the script
questions.why: The OFL reserved-name rule requires the rename to BlexMono.

questions.q: Where does install.sh copy the fonts?
questions.why: Fonts go to the user's ~/.local/share/fonts/ folder.

questions.q: On the default Gnome desktop, where do you select the Nerd Font?
questions.why: Set the profile font in gnome-terminal Preferences.

questions.q: What command runs the provided installer for the IBM Plex Mono set?
questions.why: ./install.sh IBMPlexMono installs and registers that font family.
